Home / Carbon & Climate / IREC Certification
Carbon & Climate · Category 02

IREC Renewable Energy
Certificate
Scope 2 Compliance

International Renewable Energy Certificates (I-RECs) verify that 1 MWh of electricity was generated from a renewable source and has not been counted twice. I-RECs are the GHG Protocol’s market-based instrument for Scope 2 electricity claims — required by CDP and ESG frameworks. We manage the complete I-REC lifecycle.

Per certificate 1 MWh
Market-based Scope 2 GHG Protocol
CDP compliant
I-REC Standard Global standard
I-REC Standard
Renewable Electricity
1 MWh
Per certificate
CDP
Compliant
India
Active market
1 MWh
Per I-REC certificate
CDP
Required for market-based Scope 2
I-REC
Standard body
India
Active I-REC market
Why it matters

Renewable energy claims
require traceable evidence.

Under the GHG Protocol’s market-based accounting method, your Scope 2 renewable electricity claim is only valid if backed by a formally retired energy attribute certificate. An I-REC is that evidence.

01
CDP Market-based Scope 2
CDP requires companies disclosing Scope 2 to report both location-based and market-based emissions. Market-based requires contractual instruments — PPAs, green tariffs or energy attribute certificates. I-RECs are the recognised instrument for India.
02
GHG Protocol Compliance
The GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance requires energy attribute certificates that meet specific quality criteria: issued for the same reporting period, retired exclusively on behalf of the company, and from a system that prevents double-counting. I-RECs satisfy all criteria.
03
Supply Chain RE100 Alignment
Companies that are RE100 members or that supply RE100 companies must demonstrate 100% renewable electricity through verifiable instruments. I-RECs are accepted by RE100 as a qualifying instrument for markets where direct PPAs are not available.
What we do

Complete I-REC management —
from issuance to retirement

We manage every step of the I-REC process for both energy generators (issuance) and energy buyers (procurement and retirement).

Generator Registration & Issuance
For renewable energy generators, we manage the complete I-REC issuance process — generator registration on the I-REC Standard registry, device registration, meter data submission and certificate issuance.
Buyer Procurement Strategy
For energy buyers, we design an I-REC procurement strategy aligned to your Scope 2 target, CDP reporting requirements and RE100 membership obligations — determining volume, vintage, technology mix and country of origin.
Market-based Scope 2 Calculation
We calculate your market-based Scope 2 emissions using I-REC retirement data and residual emission factors — producing the CDP-compliant, GHG Protocol-aligned market-based Scope 2 disclosure.
Official Retirement
We execute formal I-REC retirement in the I-REC registry, generating the retirement certificates that substantiate your renewable electricity claims in CDP, GHG Protocol and BRSR disclosures.
Annual Reporting Integration
I-REC retirement data is integrated into your annual GHG inventory, CDP Climate response and BRSR electricity reporting — ensuring consistency across all disclosure channels.
Our process

How we manage your I-REC programme

A four-phase programme covering both generator registration and buyer procurement, from registration through to verified retirement.

→
01
Scope 2 Analysis
Calculate your electricity consumption, identify renewable versus non-renewable sources and determine the I-REC volume required for your Scope 2 target.
→
02
Procurement or Issuance
Source I-RECs from generators or register your own facility for issuance — aligned to your CDP vintage, technology and geography requirements.
→
03
Verification & Retirement
Execute formal I-REC retirement in the registry, generate retirement certificates and verify against your electricity consumption for CDP and GHG Protocol.
04
Disclosure Integration
Integrate retirement data into GHG inventory, CDP Scope 2 response, BRSR electricity section and investor communications.
Key deliverables

What you receive from
every I-REC engagement

I-REC Retirement Certificates
Formal I-REC retirement certificates from the I-REC Standard registry — the legal documentation of your renewable electricity claims.
Market-based Scope 2 Calculation
GHG Protocol market-based Scope 2 calculation using I-REC retirement data and residual emission factors, in CDP-compliant format.
Registry Records
Public I-REC registry records providing permanent documentation of certificate issuance, transfer and retirement for investor and auditor verification.
CDP Scope 2 Integration
Formatted Scope 2 data for CDP Climate questionnaire, distinguishing location-based and market-based methods per CDP requirements.
RE100 Compliance Documentation
Documentation package demonstrating RE100 qualifying instrument retirement for RE100 members and their supply chain participants.
Annual Procurement Strategy
12-month I-REC procurement plan aligned to your renewable electricity targets, CDP disclosure schedule and budget.

Frameworks & Standards

The recognised instrument for
market-based renewable electricity

I-RECs satisfy the GHG Protocol, CDP and RE100 requirements for market-based Scope 2 — the globally recognised standard for renewable energy claims in India.

I-REC Standard GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance CDP Climate Module RE100 Initiative BRSR Renewable Energy GRI 302 Energy SBTi Scope 2 Guidance ISO 14064 Market-based
FAQ

Frequently asked
questions

Location-based Scope 2 uses the average emission factor of the electricity grid you are connected to — it reflects the carbon intensity of the physical grid. Market-based Scope 2 uses contractual instruments — PPAs, green tariffs, or energy attribute certificates like I-RECs — to reflect the specific electricity source your organisation has contracted. If you have retired I-RECs for renewable electricity you have purchased or generated, your market-based Scope 2 can be zero even if the grid is coal-heavy. CDP requires both to be reported; most investors focus on market-based.

No — they are different instruments. An I-REC (energy attribute certificate) documents the renewable origin of 1 MWh of electricity — it addresses Scope 2 electricity emissions by proving your electricity came from a clean source. A carbon credit documents the avoidance or removal of 1 tonne of CO2 equivalent — it is used to offset emissions that have already occurred. They have different registries, standards and accounting treatments. I-RECs reduce your market-based Scope 2 to zero for the electricity covered; carbon credits reduce your residual emissions after you have achieved deep reduction.

If you own or operate on-site solar generation, you can use that electricity as market-based Scope 2 without I-RECs — provided you retain the attribute (i.e., do not sell or transfer the certificate rights). If you sell the electricity or certificates, you must purchase I-RECs separately to make a market-based claim. For off-site renewable energy — which accounts for most corporate renewable electricity — I-RECs or PPAs are the required instrument. We help you design the most cost-effective combination of on-site generation and I-REC procurement for your Scope 2 target.
